reviews.review_title: Fire game, very satisfying to speed run
reviews.review_description: I got this game like a month ago and after beating the it for my first time I thought it would be interesting to speed run. And I was very impressed to see that there are many areas in this game that have skips that can shave your time significantly. My first play through took around 1hr and 10 min but now I have found new tech and about 10 major skips and my current best sits at 4.06 minutes. 10/10 game, highly recommend.
THIS GAME IS SO GOOD. Maybe add more paddles you can BUY with in game money
yes
Great game had lots of fun

Salmon Man is a hidden gem.
At first glance, it looks simple, but the deeper you go, the more you realize how much craft is behind it.

The game is built around skill, physics, and yes… frustration ... Every obstacles, every level feels carefully tuned. You can feel the amount of work that went into making the physics both demanding and fair.

It’s precise, challenging, and incredibly satisfying once you start mastering it. A deceptively simple VR platformer that reveals real depth over time.
If you enjoy physics-based gameplay that rewards patience and mastery, Salmon Man is absolutely worth your time

reviews.review_title: I don’t believe some of the reviews
reviews.review_description:
It’s too frustrating to really be fun for me.
Maybe I’m missing something but it’s just not fun unfortunately paddle hits too many objects, no control when you hit your paddle on anything, I will give any game a go but I can’t go back into this anymore.
Clearly other people love it and that’s great carry on having fun. I will raise my score to 3 for the dev.

reviews.review_title: Beat the game and this is honest
reviews.review_description:
Ive beaten the game already so take my word for it and not the dev sponsored reviews here. This is the real one. This is one of the most poorly developed games I’ve ever seen so if you’re looking for quality or polish this is NOT it. The game is frequently glitchy, and the paddle mechanics are almost NEVER consistent. Once you get to the 2nd area everything just falls apart. MASSIVE frame rate drops, buggy/stopping mid air physics breaking mechanics. I mean this thing is just a dumpster it’s freaking trash. If you’re reading this review and considering buying it please DONT!!! The devs might respond with some AI damage control response but take it from
Someone who’s gone through this game. Stay away like this thing is trash. Devs if you’re reading fix your game, this is an embarrassment and there’s a reason why it’s dead on arrival and you’ve made like 5k at most. Also find better beta testers instead of a bunch of YES men or woman whoever your friends are won’t be honest with you

Really loving this game. However ran into some issues. First, left handed mode doesn’t stay active when you quit the game and hit continue. It’ll say it’s on but I have to toggle it off and on to get it to go back on. Also, I had to start over multiple times because the continue button put me somewhere outside of the game world. Would also be nice to have a restart checkpoint option in the pause menu, instead of having to quit back to the main menu and hit continue.

Edit: checkpoint save system seems to be fixed! Still would love if you could fix the left handed thing.

Salmon Man looks simple on the surface, but it quickly reveals a surprising amount of depth once you start playing. The paddle interaction feels physical and consistent, and once you understand how it behaves against surfaces, movement becomes intentional rather than chaotic. The physics are challenging, but they feel fair, and the level design reinforces that by rewarding control instead of speed. This is a well-crafted VR experience built around a single strong idea, executed with care, and definitely worth playing if you enjoy skill-based, physics-driven gameplay.

I dont normally review things so quickly but people giving this lower than 5 stars because it doesn't have smooth turning (yet) need to realise with so few reviews it can be harmful to a new title getting the attention it deserves!

This game is great! The paddle works incredibly well and its super responsive to your hand movements - there's no lag. I had a bit of jitter when I first tried it but my Quest was running slow, a restart fixed this! No jitter after the restart.

When you're in the water you can paddle like you're rafting (but without it turning the camera) and when you push against rocks and things its a bit like doing a jump in Gorilla Tag and similar games - except its the whole paddle: you can balance and push yourself off any part of the paddle. Except unlike other games that use springy physics this locks you like you're climbing...with the whole paddle not just your hand...  it's hard to describe but its a new fresh take on these kind of games and I love exploring new mechanics in VR - and its great to play a game like this that isn't 'wobbly'.

I'm expecting this one will be really popular and a hit for all ages. Beautiful graphics and fantastic level design!

Salmon Man is a great indie game where you have to climb upstream like a salmon… but using a paddle 🛶🐟 You can even switch to two paddles if you want to make your life a bit easier (and trust me, you might!).

The game is colorful, fun, and nicely varied, with levels that keep you engaged and always pushing forward. The best twist comes after you finish a level: the water turns into lava, and suddenly not a single mistake is allowed 😅🔥

It’s surprisingly addictive, very well made, and genuinely fun to play.
Definitely worth a try! 🎮✨

I don't normally like to delete my previous reviews, but for this game I'll make an exception, the devs have done a fabulous job in making a quick repair to the save/continue function and I can now happily (although it took me an hour of swearing to get back to where I was previously) that the 'Good Luck Getting Over It' location and the 'Temple Beyond the Turtles' location both provide perfect save/continue points, now to try and get further... How have people finished this in under 3 minutes??? surely those are just the times from the original demo!

My right arm is hurting, I almost crashed a glass, and I swimmed with the fishes a lot of time 😂. 

This is a great rage quit game where you blame yourself rather then the game. There are tons of possibilities for speedrunning in this game.

Also great physic! Maybe too good physics, as you try to scratch your nose and end up falling down and need to climb up again😅.

If you like this kind of games, this is definitely a buy.

Salmon Man is a great game. The physics feels incredibly fluid, and the level design does a great job of fueling that "just one more jump" curiosity. I’m already eyeing the leaderboards for a speedrun attempt. Highly recommended! Just make sure your wrist straps are tight, or you might send your controller flying during the more "challenging" sections.😉

developer_response.body: Haha thank you!! 😄 Tight straps are definitely safer to play and if you notice anything we could improve let us know

reviews.review_title: It’s an excellent game, if you enjoy GOI
reviews.review_description: It’s incredibly enjoyable! While there might be something even better, such as the movement, the map and gameplay are incredibly fun. I highly recommend purchasing this game now, regardless of whether you’re undecided between PCVR or just Quest!

reviews.review_title: Came back to the game, black screen
reviews.review_description: I can hear myself moving and the paddle hitting stuff but it will have no visuals. I give up on games that do this.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for letting us know about the bug, we actually were able to fix this one thanks to you as it was a specific case. I'm sorry it happened but we'd be glad to have you back on our leaderboards :)

reviews.review_description:
Finally i made it up to the turtles. 
There massive frameratedrops sometimes even
short freezes. After i came back to the game and continued i just felt trough the map.
Happend more than once, so i can start from beginning again. 
No thanks

developer_response.body:
Sorry this happened! We did have an issue in our save system, but we just released a new update that fixed this problem! 
We tried to resolve it as quickly as possible because we know how frustrating losing progress can be.

If you feel like giving it another try, we’d really love to hear your feedback on the rest of the game. And thanks again for reporting it!

reviews.review_description: can you make it that reset button says new game because i tried to restart the level because i was in the void so i pressed reset and i ended up losing all of my progress. please fix this

developer_response.body: Hey thanks so much for playing and the review! Sorry to hear the restart issue, where did you end up falling in the void ? Adding a "checkpoint" system would fix that so we might need to consider this aswell. :)

reviews.review_description: Delightfully frustrating in all the best ways. Can't wait to watch speedrun videos of this!

developer_response.body: Hey Thomas you are our first review thank you so much it means a lot!

reviews.review_description: Solid game, but GOATED to speedrun. Probably my 3rd most played VR game after BeatSaber and Eleven Table Tennis
Ynueohoh
best game ever

Ive only just started this game but as a big fan if clamb i love this. Like most 'rage games' theres a hard learning curve but very satisfying and fulfilling. One of my favorite vr games at the moment!

reviews.review_title: Very good game, fun to speedrun
reviews.review_description: Very great game, was very fun to get better at and find cool routes to take. I definitely recommend and hope that there will be more map expansions in the future
this game is really fun except if you get very far and fall for the rest it is one of the best games on the meta store for the price 5 stars
Very good

Ha, this game is fun I’ve got probably 3/4 to the first course and I had to give up, the is certainly a skill to it, which I picked up pretty quickly. But boy one little tap of the paddle in the wrong place your doomed 😅

Proper fun little parkour type game with a twist! 

Nice work

This is a charming VR rendition to the genre of rage games. The game can't be simpler: you are holding an oar and have to move upstream by pushing with it. If you miss, you fall and you are dragged by the stream and have to repeat all over again.
I find it a bit easier and more forgiving to other titles of this genre that I have tried. I enjoy the genre because I'm the kind of person who enjoyed climbing stuff in Bonelab using crowbars.
It needs further polishing: 
- we need smooth turn and we need some further work on the pivoting point when turning. 
- ideally I'd like the pivoting point to be the tip of the oar if it is touching something, otherwise it is very risky to turn.
- there are frame drops every now and then and they really hurt the gameplay because they can make you miss your jump.
Overall a great title with an excellent price point.

That was a blast, can be frustrating but equally rewarding.

There’s just the one level though so hopefully they can keep expanding the game and bringing new or longer levels. 

I love space so a space area would be super cool to get to! The one section that felt medieval was super cool, would’ve loved it to be longer. Some sections seemed a bit cheap or too difficult so maybe some fine tuning but nothing impossible.

I hope to see the game expand more but am enjoying the content I have for now. I’d recommend the buy if you like platforming and a game that has you focused the entire time.
I had a lot of fun playing, I really like the art style and levels. Some parts were challenging, but I was able to make it through. Great work!
Imagine Getting Over It in VR but even more fun! Definitely would recommend!
I did not know I needed this but I could not stop laughing while playing. Soooo good !
You cannot ask more from a VR game.
